# Unity Master Skill Library

## 概述

本扩展当前提供 **34 个专业技能 + 12 个子代理**，覆盖关卡抽取、试玩广告制作、游戏开发工作流、开发进化分析、AI 资产生成、AI 资产生产、UI 系统、环境渲染、游戏机制九大领域，为 Unity 开发提供全方位专家指导。同时内置 **40+ 篇** 调试经验文档、试玩广告模板和常用代码模板。

## 技能分类

### 关卡抽取 (Level Extract)

从 Unity 项目中抽取指定关卡，移除网络/广告/统计/存档/远程配置等依赖，生成纯单机试玩广告版本。

| Skill                     | 名称               | 说明                                                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `level-extract-planner`   | 关卡抽取规划器     | 根据需求文档分析项目结构，生成模块化执行计划（ExecutionPlan.md），将改造拆解为独立模块 |
| `level-extract-developer` | 关卡抽取开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的 ExecutionPlan.md，逐模块执行改造，每模块通过编译门禁并 git commit  |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                 | 说明                                        |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `editor-plan-agent`   | 改造规划子代理 - 生成/更新 ExecutionPlan.md |
| `editor-module-agent` | 改造执行子代理 - 执行具体模块并通过编译门禁 |

适用场景：试玩、抽关、关卡抽取、playable ads、微信试玩、抖音试玩、单关卡版本、离线版本

**工作流**：

```text
用户提供需求文档 → level-extract-planner → ExecutionPlan.md → level-extract-developer → 改造完成
```

### 试玩广告制作 (Playable Ad)

| Skill                   | 名称               | 说明                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `playable-ad-planner`   | 试玩广告规划器     | 根据游戏类型和现有素材，规划 30 秒试玩广告的完整流程（开始页→游戏玩法→结算页），生成资产需求清单和开发教程   |
| `playable-ad-developer` | 试玩广告开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，优先使用现有资产，无可用资产时调用 TJGenerator 生成，快速实现 30 秒试玩广告体验 |

### 游戏开发工作流 (Game Dev Workflow)

| Skill                | 名称           | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------- | -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `game-dev-planner`   | 游戏教程规划器 | 根据游戏类型生成 AI 可执行的开发教程和资产需求清单，交接给 developer |
| `game-dev-developer` | 游戏开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，先生成资产再直接开发游戏                |
| `game-dev-debugger`  | 游戏调试执行器 | 检索开发经验知识库，为游戏开发/生成过程中的调试问题提供支持          |

### 开发进化分析 (Game Evolution)

| Skill              | 名称           | 说明                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `log-analyzer`     | 日志分析器     | 从开发会话日志中提取 bug、失败模式和可复用经验，去重后自动生成经验文档写入知识库                  |
| `session-profiler` | 会话性能分析器 | 解析聊天日志，统计工具调用次数与失败率、Skill 激活、Sub-Agent 调度、耗时分布，生成 Profiling 报告 |

### AI 资产生成 (TJGenerators)

集成团结 AI 平台的多模态生成能力，无缝嵌入 Unity 编辑器工作流。

| Skill                            | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `generate_3d_model`              | 3D 模型生成        | 生成静态 3D 模型；默认优先 Tripo P1，高精度 / PBR / FBX 场景可切换腾讯混元 3D |
| `generate_animated_character`    | 动画角色生成       | 生成带骨骼动画的 Humanoid 角色                                       |
| `generate_rigged_animated_model` | 绑骨动画模型生成   | 为现有模型绑骨和/或生成动作动画                                      |
| `generate_sprite`                | 精灵生成           | 生成 2D 精灵资产（图标、物品图、角色肖像等）                         |
| `generate_sprite_sequence`       | 精灵序列生成       | 从角色参考图生成 2D 逐帧动画                                         |
| `generate_image`                 | 图片生成           | 生成 Texture2D / PNG 图片资产（概念图、UI 背景等）                   |
| `generate_material`              | 材质生成           | 生成 PBR 表面材质（无缝纹理 PNG + `.mat` 文件）                      |
| `generate_skybox`                | 天空盒生成         | 生成天空盒 Cubemap / Skybox 材质                                     |
| `generate_audio_clip`            | 背景音乐生成       | 生成 BGM 和环境音频片段                                              |
| `generate_sound_effect`          | 音效生成           | 生成一次性音效（枪声、脚步声、爆炸等）                               |
| `generate_terrain`               | 地形生成           | 生成大尺度 Unity Terrain 地形并应用到当前场景                        |
| `generate_video`                 | 视频生成           | 生成视频资产（宣传视频、转场视频、场景视频等）                       |
| `search_assets`                  | 资产搜索下载       | 从云端资产库搜索并下载现成资产（搜索→评估→下载）                     |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                                  | 说明                                         |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `ai-generation-planner`                | AI 生成规划子代理 - 多任务生成请求拆解与执行规划 |
| `3d-model-generator`                   | 3D 模型生成子代理                            |
| `animated-character-generator`         | 动画角色生成子代理                           |
| `audio-generator`                      | 音频生成子代理                               |
| `image-generator`                      | 图片生成子代理                               |
| `material-generator`                   | 材质生成子代理                               |
| `skybox-generator`                     | 天空盒生成子代理                             |
| `sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator` | 精灵及序列生成子代理                         |
| `search-assets`                        | 资产搜索与下载子代理                         |
| `video-generator`                      | 视频生成与 VideoPlayer 配置子代理            |

### AI 资产生产 (AI Asset Production)

面向已有资产的接线、放置、地形/骨骼后处理与场景整合。

| Skill                    | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| ------------------------ | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `fbx-humanoid-auto-rig`  | FBX Humanoid 自动绑骨 | 使用 UniRig 为任意人形 FBX 自动生成骨架、Humanoid 命名、Skin 与 UV merge |
| `place_assets_in_scene`  | 场景放置专家       | 将已有资产按自然语言要求放入当前场景，支持 Prefab / Sprite / Audio / Material / TerrainData 等 |

### UI 系统 (UI Systems)

| Skill              | 名称            | 说明                                                  |
| ------------------ | --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `ui-system-router` | UI 系统路由器   | 判断合适的 UI 系统并路由到正确的专业技能              |
| `imgui`            | IMGUI 专家      | 为编辑器扩展和调试工具生成即时模式 GUI 代码           |
| `ugui`             | uGUI 专家       | 理解现有 UI、进行针对性编辑、生成新的 Canvas 层级结构 |
| `ui-toolkit`       | UI Toolkit 专家 | 生成 UXML/USS 文件、Manipulators、处理 UI 运行时绑定  |

### 环境渲染 (Environment & Rendering)

| Skill           | 名称                 | 说明                                            |
| --------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `cinemachine`   | Cinemachine 3.1 专家 | 使用模块化组件系统创建正确的摄像机设置          |
| `lighting`      | Lighting 专家        | 为 Built-in 和 URP 管线创建、配置和排查场景光照 |
| `scene-creator` | Scene Creator 专家   | 以编程方式构建、验证和修复 2D/3D 场景           |

### 游戏机制 (Gameplay Mechanics)

| Skill          | 名称             | 说明                               |
| -------------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `2d-character` | 2D 角色专家      | 端到端处理角色创建工作流           |
| `auto-wire`    | 资产自动接线专家 | 自动将生成的资产接入用户项目       |
| `input-system` | 输入系统专家     | 确定合适的系统并实现健壮的输入设置 |

## 经验知识库

### 关卡抽取需求文档 (experience/templates-level-extract/)

| 模板           | 说明                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `LevelExtract` | 关卡抽取试玩广告的标准需求文档模板，包含业务需求约束：纯单机、无存档、无广告、无统计、无远程配置 |

### 试玩广告模板 (experience/templates-playable-ad/)

| 模板                    | 说明                     |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `playable_start_page`   | 标题、按钮动画、场景切换 |
| `playable_guide_system` | 目标点、箭头、完成检测   |
| `playable_end_page`     | 分数展示、重玩按钮、微信通知 |

### 调试经验 (experience/development/)

当前内置 **30+ 篇** Unity 开发调试经验文档，涵盖常见问题：

- **UI 问题**：UGUI 遮挡 Camera、EventSystem 缺失导致点击失效、中文/字体显示异常
- **物理问题**：CapsuleCollider 中心漂移、Collider 未设 `isTrigger`、Player 插入地面
- **编译错误**：DLL 访问冲突、Library 缓存错误、匿名类型数组 `CS0826`、私有方法访问
- **渲染问题**：Sprite 半透明边缘、粒子材质显示方块、Standard Shader 光泽度无效
- **运行时问题**：场景对象被禁用、自动射击未实现、相机跟随抖动、游戏状态管理缺失

### 代码模板 (experience/templates/)

| 模板                 | 说明       |
| -------------------- | ---------- |
| `asset_loader`       | 资产加载器 |
| `camera_follow`      | 相机跟随   |
| `collectible_system` | 收集系统   |
| `procedural_meshes`  | 程序化网格 |
| `ui_panels`          | UI 面板    |

## 使用方式

```bash
# 激活特定技能
activate_skill("<skill-name>")

# 关卡抽取
activate_skill("level-extract-planner")
activate_skill("level-extract-developer")

# 试玩广告
activate_skill("playable-ad-planner")
activate_skill("playable-ad-developer")

# 游戏开发工作流
activate_skill("game-dev-planner")
activate_skill("game-dev-developer")
activate_skill("game-dev-debugger")

# AI 资产生成
activate_skill("generate_3d_model")
activate_skill("generate_animated_character")
activate_skill("generate_rigged_animated_model")
activate_skill("generate_sprite")
activate_skill("generate_sprite_sequence")
activate_skill("generate_image")
activate_skill("generate_material")
activate_skill("generate_skybox")
activate_skill("generate_audio_clip")
activate_skill("generate_sound_effect")
activate_skill("generate_terrain")
activate_skill("generate_video")
activate_skill("search_assets")

# AI 资产生产
activate_skill("fbx-humanoid-auto-rig")
activate_skill("place_assets_in_scene")

# 开发进化
activate_skill("log-analyzer")
activate_skill("session-profiler")

# Unity 专家
activate_skill("lighting")
activate_skill("cinemachine")
activate_skill("scene-creator")
activate_skill("ui-system-router")
```


## Available Sub-Agents
Use `task` for complex tasks requiring specialized analysis.

- **explore**: Fast read-only codebase search agent for finding files, patterns, and answering questions about the codebase. Use when you need to quickly find files by patterns, search code for keywords, or understand how parts of the codebase work. Specify thoroughness: 'quick' for basic searches, 'medium' for moderate exploration, 'very thorough' for comprehensive analysis.
- **general-purpose**: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you.
- **plan**: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs.
- **3d-model-generator**: Generates static (non-animated) 3D models in Unity using AI (defaults to Tripo P1; use Hunyuan 3.1 for high-poly / PBR / FBX output), then places the prefab into the scene
- **ai-generation-planner**: Planning agent for MULTI-TASK AI generation requests in Unity. Only invoke when the user's request involves TWO OR MORE distinct AI generation tasks (e.g. 'generate a character AND a skybox AND a material'). Do NOT invoke for a single generation task — call the appropriate specialist agent directly instead. When invoked, analyzes requirements, decomposes into sub-tasks, resolves dependencies, and produces a structured execution plan.
- **animated-character-generator**: Generates rigged humanoid 3D characters with animations in Unity using Meshy AI, with automatic rotation correction to ensure the character stands upright and faces the camera
- **audio-generator**: Generates background music (BGM) and sound effects (SFX) in Unity using AI, correctly configures AudioSource settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder audio files
- **image-generator**: Generates image assets (Texture2D PNG) in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images. Supports 火山 SeeDream (default, with size/segmentation options) and Frontier (stylized effects). Use this agent for general-purpose image generation — concept art, UI backgrounds, textures, posters, banners — NOT for game sprites with transparent backgrounds (use sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator instead).
- **material-generator**: Generates PBR surface material assets (seamless PNG texture + Unity .mat file) in Unity using Huoshan SeeDream AI from text prompts, reference images, or material type presets
- **search-assets**: Searches and downloads pre-made Unity assets from the cloud library, then places them in the scene. Use for '搜索资产', 'find a cat model', 'download a spaceship prefab', 'search the asset library for furniture', or any request to find/download existing Unity assets rather than generating new ones.
- **skybox-generator**: Generates Cubemap skybox assets in Unity using Rodin AI from text prompts or reference images, with automatic scene assignment via placeholder workflow
- **sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator**: Handles ALL 2D sprite and sprite sequence tasks in Unity. Three modes — choose based on inputs: Mode A (Sprite only): user wants a static 2D sprite (icon, portrait, UI element, item image), no animation needed; Mode B (Sequence only): user wants a sprite sequence animation AND already has an existing image (image_path is provided); Mode C (Sprite → Sequence): user wants a sprite sequence animation but has NO existing image — generate the sprite first, wait for completion, then use the result as image_path for generate_sprite_sequence. Always use this agent for any combination of sprite and/or sprite sequence generation.
- **video-generator**: Generates video assets in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images, correctly configures VideoPlayer settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder video files
- **editor-module-agent**: 通用 Unity 项目改造 MODULE 子代理 - 按模块使用 Unity 工具与必要的脚本改动完成任务，在不违反 global_requirements_md 约束的前提下通过 Console focus→clear→read 门禁确保编译 0 error，并用 complete_task 回传 handoff_json
- **editor-plan-agent**: 通用 Unity 项目改造 PLAN 子代理 - 严格依照 global_requirements_md 生成模块化 ExecutionPlan.md


## Task Tool (Subagent Dispatch)

The `task` tool is the primary way to launch subagents for complex, multi-step tasks.
- For a single straightforward delegation, call `task` directly without creating a job.
- When a task corresponds to an existing planned job, pass `job_id` so the Task and Job systems stay linked.
- For simple, directed codebase searches (e.g. for a specific file/class/function), use `glob` or `search_file_content` directly.
- For broader codebase exploration and deep research, use the `task` tool with subagent_name="explore". It is faster and read-only. Use this when a simple search proves insufficient or when your task requires more than 3 queries.
- When the user asks to design, architect, or plan before implementing, use the `task` tool with subagent_name="plan" — it explores the codebase and returns a step-by-step plan with critical files.

messages.content: Got it. Thanks for the context!

The user wants me to analyze the conversation history and provide a summary. Let me compile all the key information about what has been done so far in this project.## 项目总结

### 项目目标
将现有塔防游戏改造为**多玩法聚合游戏**，架构为：
- **家园养成**（主 Hub）
- **塔防玩法**（已有项目，作为扩展模块）
- **消消乐**（新建模块）

### 已完成的工作

#### Phase 1: 共享基础设施 ✅
在 `Assets/_Shared/` 下创建完整共享代码框架：

| 文件 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| `Core/Singleton.cs` | 泛型单例基类，DontDestroyOnLoad |
| `Core/EventBus.cs` | 实例化事件总线 + 全局 GlobalEventBus |
| `Core/SceneLoader.cs` | Additive 场景加载/卸载管理器 |
| `Core/GameModule.cs` | 游戏模块基类 |
| `Core/ModuleManager.cs` | 模块注册/激活/停用管理 |
| `Core/GameBootstrapper.cs` | 游戏启动器 |
| `Player/PlayerProfile.cs` | 统一玩家档案（金币/钻石/体力/等级/经验） |
| `Save/SaveSystem.cs` | 统一存档系统 |
| `Save/GameSaveData.cs` | 存档数据结构 |

#### Phase 2: 家园养成系统 ✅

**代码框架（10个脚本）：**

| 文件 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| `Building/BuildingData.cs` | 建筑 ScriptableObject 配置（类型/等级/产出/传送门目标） |
| `Building/BuildingBase.cs` | 建筑基类（初始化/升级/拆除/出售回收60%）— **已去除网格依赖** |
| `Building/BuildingManager.cs` | 建筑列表管理+放置/拆除/存档 — **已改为自由放置，无网格** |
| `Building/PortalBuilding.cs` | 传送门建筑（点击进入小游戏）— **已修复脉冲缩放bug** |
| `Building/PortalEffectController.cs` | 传送门特效脉冲控制脚本 |
| `Resource/ResourceType.cs` | 资源枚举 |
| `Resource/ResourceBuilding.cs` | 资源建筑（自动积累/收集/离线收益） |
| `Resource/ResourceManager.cs` | 木/石/食物管理，一键收集 |
| `Core/HomeManager.cs` | 家园主控制器（初始化→存档→放置→模块注册） |
| `UI/HomeMainUI.cs` | 资源栏+收集按钮+建造菜单 |

**AI 生成视觉资产（7个）：**

| 资产 | 类型 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| 金矿精灵 | Sprite | 资源产出建筑（金币） |
| 伐木场精灵 | Sprite | 资源产出建筑（木材） |
| 采石场精灵 | Sprite | 资源产出建筑（石料） |
| 塔防传送门精灵 | Sprite | 蓝紫色魔法门 |
| 消消乐传送门精灵 | Sprite | 粉红色魔法门 |
| 魔法喷泉精灵 | Sprite | 中央装饰建筑 |
| 家园背景图 | Texture2D | 卡通村庄草地背景 |

**BuildingData 配置资产（6个）：**
- 金矿/伐木场/采石场（各3级升级配置）
- 塔防传送门（targetModuleId: "towerdefense"）
- 消消乐传送门（targetModuleId: "match3"）
- 魔法喷泉（纯装饰）

**传送门特效：**

| 传送门 | 特效 |
|--------|------|
| 🌀 塔防 | 漩涡粒子（蓝紫旋转吸入+大小渐变+加法混合）+ 符文脉冲光晕（Light呼吸动画） |
| 💎 消消乐 | 星星喷射粒子（粉红→金色→白色+上浮摇摆+自旋）+ 彩虹光环粒子（粉→橙→青→紫圆环流动）+ 粉色光晕脉冲 |

**场景 HomeScene 包含：**
- Managers: HomeManager/BuildingManager/ResourceManager
- Canvas: ResourceBar/CollectAllBtn
- 6个初始建筑（自由放置，无网格）
- 正交相机 + AI背景 + EventSystem

### 修复的Bug
1. `BuildingManager.cs`/`ResourceBuilding.cs` 缺少 `using Sttop5.Shared.Player;` → 已修复
2. `PortalBuilding.Update()` 的 `transform.localScale = Vector3.one * pulse` 覆盖手动缩放 → 改为 `_baseScale * pulse`
3. 背景图 2048x1024 被 scale(10,8) 拉伸变形 → 调整为正确比例
4. 场景中 `--- GRID ---` 反复被编辑器脚本重建 → 注释掉 `CreateGridLines()` 调用
5. AppUI 包 `Canvas`/`Image` 类型与 `UnityEngine` 命名空间冲突 → 用 Editor 脚本绕开
6. `ResourceManager` 遍历方式不匹配 `IReadOnlyList` → 修复

### 整体进度

| 阶段 | 内容 | 状态 |
|------|------|------|
| Phase 1 | 共享基础设施 | ✅ 完成 |
| Phase 2 | 家园养成系统 | ✅ 完成 |
| Phase 3 | 重构塔防为插件模块 | ⏳ 未开始 |
| Phase 4 | 消消乐模块 | ⏳ 未开始 |
| Phase 5 | 集成联调 | ⏳ 未开始 |

### 下一步
- Phase 3: 将现有塔防项目重构为 HomeBase 的插件模块，通过传送门进入
- Phase 4: 创建消消乐模块
- Phase 5: 集成联调

Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

args.prompt:
Very thorough exploration of the tower defense codebase in C:\Users\97809\Desktop\sttop5\Assets\Scripts\. I need to understand:

1. All C# scripts and their responsibilities - read each file in Scripts/ subdirectories (Core, Data, Enemy, Hero, Map, Save, Skill, Skills, Tower, UI, Utils, Wave, AI)
2. How the game is initialized (entry point, scene setup)
3. How scenes are managed (GameScene, MainMenu)
4. The relationship between GameScene and MainMenu
5. Key manager classes and their dependencies
6. How the tower defense game references player data, save systems, etc.
7. Any references to UnityEngine.UI or Canvas setup

Also check:
- Assets/_Shared/ for the shared infrastructure code (read all files)
- Assets/_Modules/HomeBase/ for the home base module structure (read all files)
- Assets/Scenes/GameScene.scene and MainMenu.scene names

Return a comprehensive summary of:
- Each script file with its class name, key methods, and dependencies
- How the tower defense game currently starts up
- What needs to change to make it a loadable module under HomeBase
- Any potential circular dependencies or issues

response.output:
The user is asking me to analyze the conversation history and provide a summary. Let me look at what has happened in this conversation:

1. The user set up context about working in the sttop5 project directory
2. The user asked for a comprehensive exploration of the tower defense codebase
3. I performed an extensive analysis reading all 68+ C# files across the project
4. I provided a comprehensive summary covering all scripts, initialization flow, scene management, manager classes, player data/save systems, UI references, and migration requirements

The user now wants me to "analyze the conversation history and provide the summary as specified." This seems like they want me to summarize what was completed in this conversation - essentially a recap of the analysis I already provided.

Let me provide a concise summary of the findings from this conversation.# Conversation Summary: Tower Defense Codebase Analysis

## Task Completed
A comprehensive exploration of the tower defense codebase at `C:\Users\97809\Desktop\sttop5` was completed. All **68 C# scripts** in `Assets/Scripts/`, **9 scripts** in `Assets/_Shared/`, and **10 scripts** in `Assets/_Modules/HomeBase/` were read and analyzed.

## Key Findings Delivered

### Architecture
- **68 TowerDefense scripts** (Core, Data, Enemy, Hero, Map, Save, Skill, Skills, Tower, UI, Utils, Wave)
- **9 Shared infrastructure scripts** (module system, save system, player profile, scene loader)
- **10 HomeBase module scripts** (building system, resource manager, portal, UI)

### Critical Issues Identified

1. **Dual Save Systems** – `TowerDefense.Save.SaveSystem` and `Sttop5.Shared.Save.SaveSystem` co-exist with different data structures and file paths, creating a migration blocker.

2. **Dual Player Data** – `TowerDefense.Data.PlayerWallet`/`PlayerLevelSystem` vs `Sttop5.Shared.Player.PlayerProfile` compete for the same responsibility.

3. **Dual Singleton Implementations** – Two separate `Singleton<T>` base classes exist in different namespaces.

4. **Hardcoded GameObject.Find() calls** throughout UI scripts (找 "画布/塔选择面板", "火把", "背景" etc.) – will break under additive scene loading.

5. **Direct SceneManager.LoadScene() calls** in 6+ locations – must migrate to SceneLoader.LoadSceneAdditive().

6. **No GameModule subclass exists** for tower defense – required for HomeBase integration.

7. **No scene files exist** in Assets/Scenes/ – "GameScene" and "MainMenu" are referenced in code but missing from filesystem.

### Module Migration Requirements
- Create `TowerDefenseGameModule : GameModule`
- Resolve dual singleton/save/player conflicts
- Replace hardcoded GameObject.Find paths with serialized references
- Convert direct scene loads to additive loading via SceneLoader
- Ensure UI panels work when loaded additively under HomeBase

The analysis provided a complete per-file breakdown with class names, key methods, dependencies, and a clear migration roadmap.

toolCalls.description:
Launch a new agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously.

The Task tool launches specialized agents that autonomously handle complex tasks. Each agent has specific capabilities and tools available to it.

Available agents (use these names as the subagent_name value):
- explore: Fast read-only codebase search agent for finding files, patterns, and answering questions about the codebase. Use when you need to quickly find files by patterns, search code for keywords, or understand how parts of the codebase work. Specify thoroughness: 'quick' for basic searches, 'medium' for moderate exploration, 'very thorough' for comprehensive analysis. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)
- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)
- 3d-model-generator: Generates static (non-animated) 3D models in Unity using AI (defaults to Tripo P1; use Hunyuan 3.1 for high-poly / PBR / FBX output), then places the prefab into the scene (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- ai-generation-planner: Planning agent for MULTI-TASK AI generation requests in Unity. Only invoke when the user's request involves TWO OR MORE distinct AI generation tasks (e.g. 'generate a character AND a skybox AND a material'). Do NOT invoke for a single generation task — call the appropriate specialist agent directly instead. When invoked, analyzes requirements, decomposes into sub-tasks, resolves dependencies, and produces a structured execution plan. (Tools: read_file, write_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- animated-character-generator: Generates rigged humanoid 3D characters with animations in Unity using Meshy AI, with automatic rotation correction to ensure the character stands upright and faces the camera (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- audio-generator: Generates background music (BGM) and sound effects (SFX) in Unity using AI, correctly configures AudioSource settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder audio files (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- image-generator: Generates image assets (Texture2D PNG) in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images. Supports 火山 SeeDream (default, with size/segmentation options) and Frontier (stylized effects). Use this agent for general-purpose image generation — concept art, UI backgrounds, textures, posters, banners — NOT for game sprites with transparent backgrounds (use sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator instead). (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- material-generator: Generates PBR surface material assets (seamless PNG texture + Unity .mat file) in Unity using Huoshan SeeDream AI from text prompts, reference images, or material type presets (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- search-assets: Searches and downloads pre-made Unity assets from the cloud library, then places them in the scene. Use for '搜索资产', 'find a cat model', 'download a spaceship prefab', 'search the asset library for furniture', or any request to find/download existing Unity assets rather than generating new ones. (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- skybox-generator: Generates Cubemap skybox assets in Unity using Rodin AI from text prompts or reference images, with automatic scene assignment via placeholder workflow (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator: Handles ALL 2D sprite and sprite sequence tasks in Unity. Three modes — choose based on inputs: Mode A (Sprite only): user wants a static 2D sprite (icon, portrait, UI element, item image), no animation needed; Mode B (Sequence only): user wants a sprite sequence animation AND already has an existing image (image_path is provided); Mode C (Sprite → Sequence): user wants a sprite sequence animation but has NO existing image — generate the sprite first, wait for completion, then use the result as image_path for generate_sprite_sequence. Always use this agent for any combination of sprite and/or sprite sequence generation. (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- video-generator: Generates video assets in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images, correctly configures VideoPlayer settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder video files (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- editor-module-agent: 通用 Unity 项目改造 MODULE 子代理 - 按模块使用 Unity 工具与必要的脚本改动完成任务，在不违反 global_requirements_md 约束的前提下通过 Console focus→clear→read 门禁确保编译 0 error，并用 complete_task 回传 handoff_json (Tools: sequential_thinking, read_file, search_file_content, list_directory, glob, replace, write_file, todo_write, run_shell_command, unity_editor, unity_console, unity_script, unity_asset, unity_package, unity_scene, unity_gameobject, unity_menu, unity_shader, unity_bake, unity_ui_toolkit, unity_screenshot, execute_custom_tool)
- editor-plan-agent: 通用 Unity 项目改造 PLAN 子代理 - 严格依照 global_requirements_md 生成模块化 ExecutionPlan.md (Tools: sequential_thinking, read_file, search_file_content, list_directory, glob, replace, write_file, todo_write, run_shell_command, unity_editor, unity_console, unity_asset, unity_package, unity_scene, unity_gameobject, unity_menu)

Agent selection guidance:
- For codebase search, file discovery, or answering "where is X" questions, prefer the explore agent instead of general-purpose — it is faster and read-only.
- Use explore for quick searches, code pattern discovery, and understanding how parts of the codebase work.
- Use the plan agent for designing implementation strategies before writing code — it explores the codebase and returns a step-by-step plan with critical files.
- Prefer plan over general-purpose when the user asks to design, architect, or plan before implementing.
- Use general-purpose for multi-step tasks that require writing code, running commands, or performing actions beyond read-only exploration.

When using the Task tool, you must set the subagent_name parameter to one of the agent names listed above (e.g., "general-purpose").

When NOT to use the Task tool:
- If you want to read a specific file path, use the read_file or glob tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly
- If you are searching for a specific class definition like "class Foo", use the glob tool instead, to find the match more quickly
- If you are searching for code within a specific file or set of 2-3 files, use the read_file tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly
- Other tasks that are not related to the agent descriptions above

Usage notes:
- Always include a short description (3-5 words) summarizing what the agent will do
- Launch multiple agents concurrently whenever possible, to maximize performance; to do that, use a single message with multiple tool uses
- When the agent is done, it will return a single message back to you. The result returned by the agent is not visible to the user. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result.
- Optional display name: the name parameter is only for UI/tracking display. If omitted, description is used as the display name.
- You can optionally run agents in the background using the run_in_background parameter, but only for genuinely independent work. When an agent runs in the background, you will be notified when it completes.
- Foreground is the default. Use foreground when you need the agent's results before deciding the next step, especially research that informs implementation.
- Use background only when the work is genuinely independent and can safely complete later, such as long-running tests, build validation, independent audits, or non-overlapping research.
- After launching background tasks, do NOT call `task_output` to poll or block — continue with non-overlapping work or respond to the user. Only check progress if the user explicitly asks or the result was truncated.
- Never guess or summarize background task results before the `<bg_task_done ...>...</bg_task_done>` notification arrives.
- Do not duplicate work on the same files or topic as a running background task.
- If this execution corresponds to an existing planned job, pass job_id so the linked job is auto-claimed and auto-updated on completion/failure.
- IMPORTANT: After launching background tasks, do NOT call `task_output` to poll or block — continue with other work or respond to the user. When a background task finishes, a `<bg_task_done task_id="..." name="..." status="...">result</bg_task_done>` message is automatically injected into the conversation containing the task result. Use this directly, and only use `task_output` if the result was truncated or you need additional details.
- Provide clear, detailed prompts so the agent can work autonomously and return exactly the information you need.
- The agent's outputs should generally be trusted
- Clearly tell the agent whether you expect it to write code or just to do research (search, file reads, web fetches, etc.), since it is not aware of the user's intent
- If the agent description mentions that it should be used proactively, then you should try your best to use it without the user having to ask for it first. Use your judgement.
- If the user specifies that they want you to run agents "in parallel", you MUST send a single message with multiple Task tool use content blocks.

Example usage:
<example>
user: "Please refactor the authentication module to use JWT"
assistant: First let me explore the existing auth implementation.
assistant: Uses the Task tool to launch a general-purpose agent to search for and understand the current authentication code
assistant: Based on the exploration results, I'll now implement the JWT refactoring.
</example>
<example>
user: "Where is the database connection configured?"
assistant: Uses the Task tool to launch a general-purpose agent to find database configuration files
</example>

4. No GameModule subclass for tower defense
5. Scene files referenced but potentially missing

Let me now create a detailed plan and start implementing Phase 3. Let me first verify the scene files exist and understand the current state better.</think>分析得很好。现在我来验证一下场景文件，并在规划第三阶段之前检查当前代码的更多细节。

args.content:
# Phase 3: 塔防模块化重构计划

## 目标
将现有塔防项目（`Assets/Scripts/`）重构为 HomeBase 的可加载插件模块，通过家园传送门进入，遵循 `_Shared` 共享基础设施架构。

## 核心问题

### 1. 命名空间冲突
- `TowerDefense.Core.Singleton<T>` vs `Sttop5.Shared.Core.Singleton<T>`
- `TowerDefense.Save.SaveSystem` vs `Sttop5.Shared.Save.SaveSystem`
- `TowerDefense.Core.EventBus` (static) vs `Sttop5.Shared.Core.EventBus` (instance)
- `TowerDefense.Data.PlayerWallet` / `PlayerLevelSystem` vs `Sttop5.Shared.Player.PlayerProfile`

### 2. 场景加载方式冲突
- 塔防使用 `SceneManager.LoadScene()` 直接替换场景
- 共享架构使用 `SceneLoader.LoadSceneAdditive()` Additive 加载

### 3. DontDestroyOnLoad 冲突
- 塔防的 Singleton 子类（GameManager, SaveSystem, PlayerWallet, PlayerLevelSystem, WaveManager）全部 DontDestroyOnLoad
- 在 Additive 模式下，这些单例会跨场景持续存在，造成重复或冲突

### 4. UI 硬编码引用
- 部分脚本使用 `GameObject.Find()` 查找 UI 元素
- 在 Additive 加载下，UI Canvas 层级可能变化

## 重构策略

**核心原则：最小化侵入性改动，保持塔防代码可独立运行。**

采用"适配层"模式：
1. 保留 `Assets/Scripts/` 下所有现有代码不变（或极小改动）
2. 在 `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/` 下创建模块适配代码
3. 新建 `TowerDefenseGameModule : GameModule` 作为模块入口
4. 将 GameScene 复制为模块专属场景 `TDGameScene`
5. 模块场景中不放置 DontDestroyOnLoad 的单例，改由模块管理

## 详细步骤

### Step 1: 创建模块目录结构
```
Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/
├── Core/
│   ├── TowerDefenseGameModule.cs   # GameModule 子类，模块入口
│   ├── TDGameManager.cs            # 包装 GameManager，不继承 Singleton
│   └── TDSceneBridge.cs            # 场景桥接：进入/退出塔防
├── Save/
│   └── TDSaveAdapter.cs            # 塔防存档适配器
├── Scenes/
│   └── TDGameScene.unity           # 塔防游戏场景（从 GameScene 复制）
└── UI/
    └── TDReturnButton.cs            # 返回家园按钮
```

### Step 2: 创建 TowerDefenseGameModule
- 继承 `Sttop5.Shared.Core.GameModule`
- moduleId = "towerdefense"
- sceneName = "TDGameScene"
- Activate() → 加载塔防场景，初始化塔防管理器
- Deactivate() → 清理塔防状态，卸载场景，重置 Time.timeScale
- 实现 GetModuleSaveData() / RestoreModuleSaveData()

### Step 3: 修改塔防 Singleton 行为（关键改动）
**问题**: 塔防的 Singleton 基类 `TowerDefense.Core.Singleton<T>` 执行 `DontDestroyOnLoad`，在 additive 场景下会导致单例残留。

**方案**: 在 `TowerDefense.Core.Singleton<T>` 中添加条件控制：
- 添加 `protected bool _dontDestroyOnLoad = true;` 虚属性
- Awake 中根据此标志决定是否调用 `DontDestroyOnLoad`
- 当作为模块运行时，`GameManager`、`PlayerWallet`、`PlayerLevelSystem`、`SaveSystem` 不再 DontDestroyOnLoad
- 用编译指令 `#if TD_MODULE` 控制行为切换，避免影响独立运行

**更好的替代方案**: 不改 Singleton 基类，改用 `TDSceneBridge` 在模块停用时手动 Destroy 这些单例。

### Step 4: 处理 PlayerWallet / PlayerLevelSystem 冲突
**方案**: 让塔防的 `PlayerWallet` 和 `PlayerLevelSystem` 在模块模式下代理到 `PlayerProfile`：
- 在 `GameManager.Awake()` 中，检查 `PlayerProfile.Instance` 是否存在
- 如果存在（模块模式），将 `PlayerWallet` 和 `PlayerLevelSystem` 的数据源指向 `PlayerProfile`
- 如果不存在（独立模式），使用自身数据

**具体改动**:
- `GameManager`：在 `Start()` 中检测场景名 "GameScene" vs "TDGameScene" 来判断运行模式
- `PlayerWallet`：添加 `ProxyToPlayerProfile()` 方法，转发金币/钻石操作
- `PlayerLevelSystem`：添加 `ProxyToPlayerProfile()` 方法，转发经验/等级操作

### Step 5: 处理 SaveSystem 冲突
**方案**: 
- 塔防的 `SaveSystem` 在模块模式下不自动加载/保存
- 改用 `TDSaveAdapter` 将塔防存档数据通过 `_Shared/Save/SaveSystem` 的模块化存档接口保存
- `TowerDefense.Save.SaveSystem` 的 `Awake()` 中检测共享 SaveSystem，如果存在则跳过自身初始化

### Step 6: 修改场景加载方式
**改动文件**:
- `StartGameButton.cs`: `SceneManager.LoadScene()` → 改为调用 `ModuleManager.ActivateModule("towerdefense")`
- `VictoryPanel.cs`: `SceneManager.LoadScene("MainMenu")` → 改为调用 `ModuleManager.DeactivateCurrentModule()` 返回家园
- `GameManager.RestartGame()`: `SceneManager.LoadScene(activeSceneName)` → 使用 `SceneLoader` 重载模块场景

### Step 7: 创建 TDGameScene
- 复制 GameScene 为 TDGameScene，放在 `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Scenes/`
- 在场景中添加 `TowerDefenseGameModule` 组件
- 移除场景中可能的 MainMenu 相关组件
- 确保场景可通过 Additive 模式正确加载

### Step 8: 修改 HomeScene 传送门
- `PortalBuilding` 点击时调用 `ModuleManager.Instance.ActivateModule("towerdefense")`
- 确认 BuildingData 中塔防传送门的 targetModuleId = "towerdefense"

### Step 9: 处理 Time.timeScale 问题
- 塔防使用 `Time.timeScale` 控制暂停/加速
- 模块退出时必须重置 `Time.timeScale = 1f`
- 在 `TowerDefenseGameModule.Deactivate()` 中确保重置

### Step 10: 编译验证
- 每步改动后 compile + console check
- 确保 HomeScene 和 TDGameScene 分别可运行
- 确保通过传送门进入/退出塔防流畅

## 文件改动清单

| 文件 | 改动类型 | 说明 |
|------|----------|------|
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Core/TowerDefenseGameModule.cs` | 新建 | 模块入口 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Core/TDSceneBridge.cs` | 新建 | 场景桥接 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Save/TDSaveAdapter.cs` | 新建 | 存档适配 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/UI/TDReturnButton.cs` | 新建 | 返回家园按钮 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Scenes/TDGameScene.unity` | 新建(复制) | 模块场景 |
| `Assets/Scripts/Core/GameManager.cs` | 修改 | 添加模块模式检测和代理逻辑 |
| `Assets/Scripts/Core/PlayerWallet.cs` | 修改 | 代理到 PlayerProfile |
| `Assets/Scripts/Core/PlayerLevelSystem.cs` | 修改 | 代理到 PlayerProfile |
| `Assets/Scripts/Save/SaveSystem.cs` | 修改 | 模块模式下跳过自动加载 |
| `Assets/Scripts/UI/VictoryPanel.cs` | 修改 | 返回家园用 ModuleManager |
| `Assets/Scripts/UI/StartGameButton.cs` | 修改 | 使用 ModuleManager 进入 |
| `Assets/_Modules/HomeBase/Building/PortalBuilding.cs` | 修改 | 确认传送门调用 ModuleManager |

## 执行顺序

1. ✅ 创建模块目录结构
2. ✅ 创建 TowerDefenseGameModule
3. ✅ 创建 TDSceneBridge
4. ✅ 创建 TDSaveAdapter
5. ✅ 修改 PlayerWallet（代理 PlayerProfile）
6. ✅ 修改 PlayerLevelSystem（代理 PlayerProfile）
7. ✅ 修改 SaveSystem（模块模式适配）
8. ✅ 修改 GameManager（模块模式检测）
9. ✅ 修改 VictoryPanel / StartGameButton

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to create a structured task list for your current coding session. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.
It also helps the user understand the progress of the job and overall progress of their requests.

## When to Use This Tool
Use this tool proactively in these scenarios:
- Complex multi-step tasks - When a job requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions
- Non-trivial and complex tasks - Work that requires careful planning or multiple operations
- Plan mode - When using plan mode, create a job list to track the work
- User explicitly requests job list - When the user directly asks you to use the job list
- User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)
- After receiving new instructions - Immediately capture user requirements as jobs
- When you start working on a job - Mark it as in_progress BEFORE beginning work
- After completing a job - Mark it as completed and add any new follow-up jobs discovered during implementation

## When NOT to Use This Tool
Skip using this tool when:
- There is only a single, straightforward job
- The job is trivial and tracking it provides no organizational benefit
- The job can be completed in less than 3 trivial steps
- The job is purely conversational or informational

NOTE that you should not use this tool if there is only one trivial job to do. In this case you are better off just doing the job directly.

## Job Fields
- **subject**: A brief, actionable title in imperative form (e.g., "Fix authentication bug in login flow")
- **description**: Detailed description of what needs to be done, including context and acceptance criteria
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when job is in_progress (e.g., "Fixing authentication bug"). This is displayed to the user while you work on the job.

**IMPORTANT**: Always provide activeForm when creating jobs. The subject should be imperative ("Run tests") while activeForm should be present continuous ("Running tests"). All jobs are created with status `pending`.

## Tips
- Create jobs with clear, specific subjects that describe the outcome
- Include enough detail in the description for another agent to understand and complete the job
- After creating jobs, use job_update to set up dependencies (blocks/blockedBy) if needed
- Check job_list first to avoid creating duplicate jobs
- Use `allowDuplicate: true` only when you intentionally need a second open job with the same normalized subject

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- After completing your current job, look for the next available job instead of stopping
- Available jobs are typically: status = 'pending', no owner, and no open blockedBy dependencies
- **Prefer working on jobs in ID order** (lowest ID first) when multiple jobs are available, as earlier jobs often set up context for later ones
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Continuation

- After marking a job completed, use `job_list` to check for newly unblocked work
- If a clear unblocked job exists, continue with it rather than pausing for an unnecessary decision
- If no job is available, or remaining work is genuinely blocked or ambiguous, then ask the user or update dependencies accordingly

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

toolCalls.description:
Manage Unity project assets on disk (under "Assets/") such as materials, physics materials, textures/importers, ScriptableObjects, and existing prefabs.

Key uses:
- Read: search assets, get info (optionally with preview), get components from a Prefab asset
- Write: create folders/materials/physics materials/scriptable objects; modify existing assets (including setting Prefab component properties); move/rename/duplicate assets; ensure .meta health

Important notes:
- `create` currently supports only: Folder, Material, PhysicsMaterial, ScriptableObject (Prefab creation is not supported).
- `rename` is effectively a move: pass a full destination asset path (e.g. "Assets/Foo/NewName.ext").
- `generate_preview` can return large base64 payloads; avoid unless you truly need a thumbnail.

Asset (re)import operations are heavy and should only be used when absolutely necessary. Prefer normal Unity import, ensure_*, and script recompilation instead of full reimport.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- After completing your current job, look for the next available job instead of stopping
- Available jobs are typically: status = 'pending', no owner, and no open blockedBy dependencies
- **Prefer working on jobs in ID order** (lowest ID first) when multiple jobs are available, as earlier jobs often set up context for later ones
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Continuation

- After marking a job completed, use `job_list` to check for newly unblocked work
- If a clear unblocked job exists, continue with it rather than pausing for an unnecessary decision
- If no job is available, or remaining work is genuinely blocked or ambiguous, then ask the user or update dependencies accordingly

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

toolCalls.description:
Manage Unity project assets on disk (under "Assets/") such as materials, physics materials, textures/importers, ScriptableObjects, and existing prefabs.

Key uses:
- Read: search assets, get info (optionally with preview), get components from a Prefab asset
- Write: create folders/materials/physics materials/scriptable objects; modify existing assets (including setting Prefab component properties); move/rename/duplicate assets; ensure .meta health

Important notes:
- `create` currently supports only: Folder, Material, PhysicsMaterial, ScriptableObject (Prefab creation is not supported).
- `rename` is effectively a move: pass a full destination asset path (e.g. "Assets/Foo/NewName.ext").
- `generate_preview` can return large base64 payloads; avoid unless you truly need a thumbnail.

Asset (re)import operations are heavy and should only be used when absolutely necessary. Prefer normal Unity import, ensure_*, and script recompilation instead of full reimport.

messages.content:
The script exists as an asset but the type isn't found because compilation failed. The compilation failed because `Assets/Scripts/` files reference `TowerDefense.Module` which can't resolve. It's a chicken-and-egg problem.

The fix: I should not use `TowerDefense.Module` namespace from `Assets/Scripts/`. Instead, I should change the approach - have the scripts in `Assets/Scripts/` reference the `Sttop5.Shared` types directly (which they can already access), and move the `IsModuleMode` detection to not depend on `TowerDefense.Module`.

Let me change the approach: instead of referencing `TowerDefenseGameModule.IsModuleMode` from the Scripts/ code, I'll use a simpler detection mechanism.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- After completing your current job, look for the next available job instead of stopping
- Available jobs are typically: status = 'pending', no owner, and no open blockedBy dependencies
- **Prefer working on jobs in ID order** (lowest ID first) when multiple jobs are available, as earlier jobs often set up context for later ones
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Continuation

- After marking a job completed, use `job_list` to check for newly unblocked work
- If a clear unblocked job exists, continue with it rather than pausing for an unnecessary decision
- If no job is available, or remaining work is genuinely blocked or ambiguous, then ask the user or update dependencies accordingly

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

messages.content:
The `HomeManager` registers itself as a module. The `PortalBuilding` tries to activate a module by ID, but the tower defense module won't be registered until its scene is loaded and `TowerDefenseGameModule.Activate()` is called.

The key issue: `ModuleManager.ActivateModule()` loads the module scene AND activates the module. But the current flow has a problem - when `ActivateModule("towerdefense")` is called, it tries to find the module in the registry first, and if it's not registered, it errors out.

I need to change the approach: `ModuleManager.ActivateModule()` should load the scene, and the module should self-register when the scene loads. Let me update the flow:

toolCalls.name: read_file

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. - After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- After completing your current job, look for the next available job instead of stopping
- Available jobs are typically: status = 'pending', no owner, and no open blockedBy dependencies
- **Prefer working on jobs in ID order** (lowest ID first) when multiple jobs are available, as earlier jobs often set up context for later ones
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Continuation

- After marking a job completed, use `job_list` to check for newly unblocked work
- If a clear unblocked job exists, continue with it rather than pausing for an unnecessary decision
- If no job is available, or remaining work is genuinely blocked or ambiguous, then ask the user or update dependencies accordingly

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

messages.content:
Phase 3 is complete. Let me provide a summary of all changes:

## Phase 3 完成：塔防模块化重构 ✅

### 新建文件

| 文件 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| `Assets/Scripts/Core/ModuleModeDetector.cs` | 全局静态标志，检测塔防是否以模块模式运行 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Core/TowerDefenseGameModule.cs` | `GameModule` 子类，塔防模块入口 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Core/TDSceneBridge.cs` | 场景桥接器，处理进入/退出塔防 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Save/TDSaveAdapter.cs` | 存档适配器，桥接塔防存档到共享 SaveSystem |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/UI/TDReturnButton.cs` | 返回家园按钮组件 |
| `Assets/_Modules/TowerDefense/Scenes/TDGameScene.scene` | 塔防模块场景（从 GameScene 复制） |

### 修改文件

| 文件 | 改动 |
|------|------|
| `Assets/Scripts/Core/GameManager.cs` | 添加 `_isModuleMode` 检测，模块模式下不 DontDestroyOnLoad，`RestartGame()` 使用 SceneLoader，新增 `ReturnToHome()`，GameOver 发布 `MiniGameCompletedEvent` |
| `Assets/Scripts/Core/PlayerWallet.cs` | 添加 `_isProxyMode` 代理模式，转发金币/钻石/体力操作到 `PlayerProfile` |
| `Assets/Scripts/Core/PlayerLevelSystem.cs` | 添加 `_isProxyMode` 代理模式，转发经验/等级到 `PlayerProfile` |
| `Assets/Scripts/Save/SaveSystem.cs` | 添加 `_isModuleMode` 检测，模块模式下使用共享 SaveSystem 的模块存档接口 |
| `Assets/Scripts/UI/VictoryPanel.cs` | `OnMainMenuClicked` 在模块模式下调用 `GameManager.ReturnToHome()` |
| `Assets/Scripts/UI/StartGameButton.cs` | 模块模式下使用 `ModuleManager.ActivateModule("towerdefense")` |
| `Assets/_Shared/Core/ModuleManager.cs` | 新增 `ActivateModuleByScene()` 方法，支持未注册模块通过场景加载自动注册 |
| `Assets/_Modules/HomeBase/Scripts/Building/PortalBuilding.cs` | 移除预检查模块注册的代码，直接调用 `ActivateModule` |
| `Assets/Editor/AddPortalEffects.cs` | 修复 `EditorSceneManager` 命名空间引用 |

### 架构设计

```
HomeScene (传送门点击)
    → ModuleManager.ActivateModule("towerdefense")
    → 加载 TDGameScene (Additive)
    → TowerDefenseGameModule 自动注册并激活
    → ModuleModeDetector.IsModuleMode = true
    → PlayerWallet/PlayerLevelSystem/SaveSystem 进入代理模式
    → 塔防游戏运行
    → 点击"返回家园"
    → ModuleManager.DeactivateCurrentModule()
    → 清理塔防状态，卸载 TDGameScene
    → ModuleModeDetector.IsModuleMode = false
```

### 编译状态
- **0 errors, 9 warnings**（全部为预存在的未使用字段警告）

toolCalls.description:
Manage Unity project assets on disk (under "Assets/") such as materials, physics materials, textures/importers, ScriptableObjects, and existing prefabs.

Key uses:
- Read: search assets, get info (optionally with preview), get components from a Prefab asset
- Write: create folders/materials/physics materials/scriptable objects; modify existing assets (including setting Prefab component properties); move/rename/duplicate assets; ensure .meta health

Important notes:
- `create` currently supports only: Folder, Material, PhysicsMaterial, ScriptableObject (Prefab creation is not supported).
- `rename` is effectively a move: pass a full destination asset path (e.g. "Assets/Foo/NewName.ext").
- `generate_preview` can return large base64 payloads; avoid unless you truly need a thumbnail.

Asset (re)import operations are heavy and should only be used when absolutely necessary. Prefer normal Unity import, ensure_*, and script recompilation instead of full reimport.

现在我理解了完整的问题。HomeScene 中的传送门建筑是**场景中预先放置的 GameObject**，不是通过 `BuildingManager.PlaceBuilding()` 创建的。所以它们从未调用 `Initialize()`，`_isPlaced` 一直是 `false`，`_data` 也可能没有正确赋值。

我需要做两件事：
1. 修复场景中预放置的建筑，让它们的 `_isPlaced = true` 并且 `_data` 已赋值
2. 同时修正 BuildingData 中的 `targetSceneName`

<think>The user is clicking on the portal but nothing happens. Let me focus on the core issue: `_isPlaced` is `false` in Play Mode, which causes `OnMouseDown()` to return immediately.

Let me check the BuildingBase.Update() code I modified - it should auto-set `_isPlaced = true` when `_data != null`. But the test showed it's still false. Let me look at the actual code more carefully.

The issue might be that the compile happened but the domain reload hasn't taken effect. Or the Update() code isn't running because the building is a PortalBuilding which has `new void Update()` that overrides BuildingBase.Update().

That's the bug! `PortalBuilding` has `private new void Update()` which HIDES `BuildingBase.Update()`. So the auto-fix in BuildingBase.Update() never gets called for PortalBuilding!</think>The user is asking why clicking the portal does nothing. Let me look at the key issue.

The problem: `PortalBuilding` has `private new void Update()` which **hides** `BuildingBase.Update()`, so my auto-fix in the base class never runs for portals.

thinkingSummaries.text: Fixing hidden Update method in PortalBuilding

messages.content:
Found it. `PortalBuilding.Update()` uses `new` keyword, which **hides** `BuildingBase.Update()` entirely. The base class auto-fix for `_isPlaced` never runs. Two fixes needed:

1. Call `base.Update()` from PortalBuilding's Update, or just add the auto-fix directly
2. The simplest fix: remove the `!_isPlaced` guard from `OnMouseDown` since it's blocking everything

messages.content:
<think>The user is saying they don't want the additive scene loading module approach. They want:
1. HomeScene is a standalone scene
2. Clicking the portal should load the tower defense's MainMenu scene (not GameScene directly via additive loading)
3. The tower defense game itself works as standalone scenes (MainMenu → GameScene)
4. They just want simple scene transitions: HomeScene → MainMenu (tower defense) → GameScene (tower defense)

So the whole "module" architecture with additive scene loading, GameModule, ModuleManager, SceneLoader, proxy patterns etc. is overengineered for what they actually want. They just want:
- HomeScene with portals
- Click portal → `SceneManager.LoadScene("MainMenu")` to go to tower defense
- In tower defense, play normally, then return to HomeScene

Let me simplify this dramatically. The user wants straightforward scene switching, not a complex module system.

Let me fix the PortalBuilding to just do a simple scene load to the tower defense MainMenu scene.</think>明白了！你要的是简单的场景切换：**HomeScene → 点击传送门 → 加载塔防的 MainMenu 场景**，不需要什么 Additive 加载和模块系统。

让我直接改 `PortalBuilding`，让它用最简单的 `SceneManager.LoadScene` 跳转：

thinkingSummaries.text: Simplifying scene transitions to direct loading

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

0 errors。现在的流程很简单：

```
HomeScene → 点击传送门 → SceneManager.LoadScene("MainMenu") → 塔防主菜单
塔防胜利/返回 → SceneManager.LoadScene("HomeScene") → 回家园
```

你现在可以点 Play 测试了。点击蓝色传送门会直接切换到塔防的 MainMenu 场景。
